grants.title: FY 2026 Project Safe Neighborhoods (PSN) RFA
grants.agency: Office of Victim Services and Justice Grants
grants.raw_text:
FY26 Project Safe Neighborhood (PSN) RFA  
Questions & Answers  
  
Q1: How much funding is available? What is the expected grant award size?  
A: OVSJG does not currently announce the total amount of available funds on any of its requests for 
applications. Funds are awarded based on availability and eligibility.  
Q2: Have you gotten this funding in the past or is this new funding? The RFA title says 
“Supplemental” — are these dollars left over from another year or is this new special funding?  
A: It is not special funding. It is supplemental because it is outside of our regular RFA cycle.  
Q3: How many awards do you anticipate making?  
A: The number of awards depends on how many applicants apply and meet eligibility requirements.  
Q4: Is OVSJG most interested in funding re -entry programming through this RFA or are broader 
violence prevention programs also given equal consideration?  
A: Funding is for Project Safe Neighborhoods (PSN) activities, including implementing effective programs 
and strategies that enable PSN task forces to prevent, respond to, and reduce violent crimes; identifying 
high crime areas; and fostering effective col laboration with affected communities.  
Q5: Are there specific programmatic outputs or outcomes you’re interested in seeing grantees 
address (i.e., reduced recidivism)?  
A: Please refer to Section 2. Funding Opportunity Descriptions (Page 6) and Section 3.5 Data Collection 
Requirements and Information (Page 8) of the RFA.  
Q6: Are District of Columbia Courts eligible to apply, or is it only open to community -based non -
profit agencies?  
A: Yes, District Courts can apply for the grant.  
Q7: For the financial audit requirement, what is considered the “most recent fiscal year”?  
A: If your organization operates on a calendar year (Jan 1 –Dec 31), you may submit your 2024 audit as the 
most recent fiscal year audit for this application.  
Q8: What is the amount that can be requested for this grant?  
A: OVSJG does not

and coordinate programs that improve public safety; enhance the administration of justice; 
and create systems of care for crime victims, youth, and their families in the District.Agency Overview

grants.title: FY26 Victim Services RFA Questions & Responses
grants.agency: Office of Victim Services and Justice Grants
grants.raw_text:
FY26 Victim Services RFA Questions & Responses  
 
1) I was reviewing the NOFA and RFA for the FY 2026 Consolidated Victim Services 
grants and they appear to have different application due dates. The NOFA says the 
application is due 6/16/25 and the RFA says the application is due 6/17/25. Which is 
the correct date?   
 
Application  Deadline:  11:59pm  ET June 17, 2025.  
 
2) I am looking at the RFA for our FY26 Victims' Services application and noticed the 
due date is different than the due date in the email that was circulated. The RFA 
says EOD Tuesday the 17th and the email says EOD Monday the 16th. Could you 
confirm which one is correct?  
 
Application Deadline: 11:59pm ET June 17, 2025.   
 
3) May we kindly upload this Certificate of Amendment to supplement the DC 
business license for your review and records?   
 
Yes, you can upload the Certificate of Amendment to supplement the business license.  
 
4) Under the service category section of the application in GovGrants (under 
Overview), I am not seeing Crisis Counseling and Intervention as a category to 
check. But it is listed in the RFA. Can you provide guidance?   
 
Please see description for Crisis Intervention to determine whether this fits the criteria for 
services you  provide.  Also, a ll performance measurement information can be found on 
the OVSJG performance measurement initiative website. Please note the FY26 metrics 
have not been posted yet. The FY25 metrics can be used as a guide for your application 
and selected applicants will be  notified of any updates to the metrics for FY26. 
Performance Measurement Initiative (PMI) | ovsjg   
 
5) I am wondering if you could provide any guidance on submission categories and 
appropriate first -time applicant monetary requests?  
 
Applicants (including those that are first time)  may select their funding preference based 
on the types of services they plan to provide with grant funds. However, OVSJG will determine the most appropriate fundi

grants.title: FY26 Justice Grants RFA Questions & Responses
grants.agency: Office of Victim Services and Justice Grants
grants.raw_text:
FY26 Justice Grants  RFA Questions & Responses  
 
1) Page  12 of the RFA  says to refer to the appendix for reference resources to evidence 
based practices. Please clarify where those are listed in the Appendices.  
 
That section will be amended and taken out as that information now lives on our website. 
For more resources please visit: Training and Technical Assistance | ovsjg  
 
2) Could you please give more detail about the Service Categories? It would be helpful 
to have guidance on which categories we should choose, including what kinds of 
questions are included within each category and whether certain service categories 
are requir ed for specific funding opportunities or categories within the RFA.  
 
All performanc e measurement information can be found on the OVSJG performance 
measurement i nitiative  website. Please note the FY26 metrics have not been poste d yet. 
The FY25 metrics can be used as a guide for your application and selected applicants will 
be notified of any updates to the metrics for FY26.  Performance Measurement Initiative 
(PMI) |  ovsjg  
3) I am writing to inquire about the eligibility criteria for the grant. Specifically, I 
would like to know if an organization without audited financial statements, but with 
an audit scheduled to be completed by the end of the fiscal year, is eligible to 
appl y. Additionally, could you please send me a copy of the slides and the recording  
from the information sessions related to this grant ?  
Submission of a certified letter from your accountant, on their letterhead, indicating an 
audit is scheduled for completion by 12/31/2025 along with the submission of the most 
recent audit should be included with your application.   If awarded, special cond itions will 
apply to indicate the completed audit is required prior to the end of first quarter, or 
12/31/2025 and prior to the disbursement of any reimbursements.
